posting.sourceTitle: High Rise Concrete Carpenters JATC of New York City
posting.sponsorName: Joint Apprenticeship and Training Committee for the High Rise Concrete Carpenters of New York City
posting.applicationSummary: Attend an in-person information session at the NYCDCC Training Center to receive a passcode, then complete and submit the application online within 60 days of the session.
posting.applicationHours: In-person information sessions are held on the second Wednesday of each month, excluding December and January, from 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM. No admittance after 9:00 AM.
posting.applicationLimitDetails: Spaces are limited and filled on a first-come, first-served basis.
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 17 years old when entering the apprenticeship program.
posting.educationRequirement: Must have a high school diploma or high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED; proof is required after selection and before enrollment.
posting.transportationRequirement: Must travel to off-site training and may be responsible for certain costs if they cancel, fail to show up, or do not complete the program.
posting.drugTestRequirement: Must pass a substance abuse screening within 48 hours of selection and before enrollment, and must agree to an apprentice drug testing policy with pre-enrollment, random, for-cause, and post-accident testing.
allRequirements: Must sign in during the in-person information session; failure to sign in will disqualify the applicant.
Applicants may apply for only one trade.
Applications remain on file for two years, then expire and the applicant must reapply if still interested.
Applications must be received no later than 60 days from the date of the information session.
Must be at least 17 years old when entering the apprenticeship program.
Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED; proof is required after selection and before enrollment.
Must be physically able to perform the work of the trade without posing a direct threat to the safety of themselves or others.

Must attest in writing that they are physically able to perform Carpenter work, including working on scaffolds and in confined spaces, working in inclement weather, performing constant repetitive motions, climbing and working from heights, lifting at least 50 lbs., and standing and stooping for long periods.
Must pass a substance abuse screening, at the sponsor's expense, within 48 hours of selection and before enrollment; applicants who do not pass will be disqualified.
Must agree to and sign an Apprentice drug testing policy covering pre-enrollment, random, for-cause, and post-accident testing.
Must provide proof of any trade-related experience, certification, or education after selection and before enrollment.
Must agree to and sign an Apprentice Handbook, which is subject to change at any time.
Must travel to off-site training and agree to be financially responsible for certain costs if the apprentice cancels, fails to show up, or fails to successfully complete the program.
Must be able to read, hear, and understand written and verbal instructions and warnings in English.
Must be a U.S. citizen or have the legal right to work in the United States; proof is required after selection and before enrollment.
Must provide DD-214, Certificate of Release or Discharge from Active Duty, if applicable, after selection and before enrollment.
Applications that meet all requirements will be scheduled for an interview.
Applicants are only allowed one interview, and those who do not arrive on time will be disqualified from further consideration.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be at least 17, have a high school diploma or equivalency, attend the required in-person information session, pass substance abuse screening, and meet physical, English-language, work-authorization, and training-travel requirements.
posting.contactName: High Rise Concrete Carpenters of New York City
posting.contactSummary: Contact High Rise Concrete Carpenters of New York City at (212) 727-2224 Ext. "Admissions" for more information.
